name: gmickel-claude-marketplace
description: Claude Code plugin marketplace featuring flow-next: a plan-first workflow system with .flow/ task tracking, Ralph autonomous overnight coding mode, multi-model review gates via RepoPrompt/Codex, 20 subagents, 11 commands, and 16 skills. Zero external dependencies.

dangerous_patterns: hooks/hooks.json registers PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, and SubagentStop hooks for ralph-guard.py — only active when FLOW_RALPH=1 env var is set
ralph-guard.py is a conditional guard script (not examined in full, but hook is gated by env var and file existence check)
findings_summary.notes: Mature, well-documented plugin from Gordon Mickel (mickel.tech). The flow-next plugin includes 16 SKILL.md files (browser, flow-next-deps, flow-next-epic-review, flow-next-export-context, flow-next-impl-review, etc.), 11 commands, and 20 subagents. The hooks.json guards ralph-guard.py behind two conditions: the file must exist at scripts/ralph/hooks/ralph-guard.py AND the FLOW_RALPH=1 env var must be set. This is a responsible opt-in pattern. The plugin also uses a .flow/ task tracking system with JSON epics. Extensive documentation. Active project (v0.24.0). MIT licensed. Score reflects hook presence requiring trust evaluation, but the gating mechanism and transparency earn passing status.
